title: Liquid-Cooling a TE Connectivity 800V DC Busbar and More from the Wiwynn Booth
gist: At Computex 2026, Wiwynn and TE Connectivity showcased next-generation AI server infrastructure requiring 800V DC power delivered via liquid-cooled busbars, signaling a major power density inflection point for data centers driven by the next generation of AI accelerators. The demo highlights how the entire power cable assembly must drop in as a single unit during robotic manufacturing, marking a shift in how high-power AI servers are built.

themes.theme: 800V DC Power Transition in AI Data Centers

insights.claim: The data center industry is transitioning from lower-voltage DC power to 800V DC busbars to support the dramatically higher power density demanded by next-generation AI accelerators.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Investors in data center power infrastructure, connectors, and cooling companies should expect a major upgrade cycle driven by 800V DC adoption for AI workloads.
insights.quote: there is a move to 800V DC power in the data center. Not only is the industry putting more power through an 800V busbar, but that busbar is being liquid-cooled.

insights.claim: TE Connectivity is supplying an HVDC IT GEAR TO ELCON MICRO+ power cable assembly rated at 800V/75A for the server-to-busbar interface, bridging a 400V ELCON MICRO+ end and an 800V HVDC IT gear end in a single prefabricated assembly.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: TE Connectivity is positioned as a key supplier of the high-voltage connector ecosystem for next-gen AI servers; this product represents a new revenue category.
insights.quote: TE Connectivity HVDC IT GEAR TO ELCON MICRO+ Power Cable Assembly that has an 800V/75A HVDC IT gear end and a 400V ELCON MICRO+ end, all in an assembly.

insights.claim: Liquid cooling is being extended to the 800V DC busbar itself, not just compute components, reflecting that thermal management is now a constraint at the power distribution layer in dense AI racks.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Liquid cooling vendors and busbar manufacturers face a growing addressable market as thermal management moves up the rack power stack.
insights.quote: Not only is the industry putting more power through an 800V busbar, but that busbar is being liquid-cooled.

themes.theme: Robotic Manufacturing and Modular Power Assembly Design

insights.claim: Next-generation AI server power cable assemblies are being designed as single drop-in units rather than field-assembled cables, driven by the industry's shift toward robotic manufacturing on the production line.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Connector and cable assembly suppliers who can deliver factory-ready integrated power modules will gain share over those selling raw cable and connectors separately.
insights.quote: The industry is moving to more robotic assembly, so building the power cable assembly is not really practical on the line. Instead, the entire thing is meant to drop in during manufacturing.

insights.claim: The centralized power cable assembly in next-gen AI servers is positioned between two large-footprint AI accelerator modules (each over 100 cm2), meaning the interconnect design must be co-optimized with the accelerator module layout.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Server OEM and accelerator co-design is tightening; suppliers must integrate closely with accelerator roadmaps to win socket positions.
insights.quote: Here are the two 100+cm2 accelerator dummy modules with the power assembly in the middle.

themes.theme: Wiwynn as Early-Indicator AI Infrastructure Bellwether

insights.claim: Wiwynn's Computex booth consistently previews directionally accurate next-generation data center infrastructure 12-18 months before mass deployment, making its mock-ups a leading indicator of where the industry is heading.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Investors tracking AI infrastructure trends can use Wiwynn's mock-ups as an early-warning signal for which component categories are entering high-growth phases.
insights.quote: The Wiwynn booth always showcases great technology ahead of its time, making it one of my favorites to visit.

insights.claim: Wiwynn is demonstrating ORv3-form-factor next-generation AI servers at Computex 2026, indicating that the Open Compute Project rack standard will persist into the 800V DC era rather than being displaced by proprietary form factors.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: OCP-ecosystem suppliers (rack, networking, power) have a clearer path into next-gen AI server deployments if ORv3 compatibility is maintained.
insights.quote: let us start at the front with what looks like a normal ORv3 server node.

insights.claim: The transition to next-generation AI accelerators is driving a simultaneous inflection in both compute density and per-server power requirements, necessitating an end-to-end redesign of power delivery from the busbar to the chip.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Power semiconductor, connector, and liquid cooling companies are entering a multi-year capex upgrade cycle tied directly to AI accelerator generational transitions.
insights.quote: This year, we are seeing a transition to a new generation of AI accelerators and, therefore, a new level of power density.

insights.claim: Next-generation AI servers will require pervasive internal liquid cooling blocks on all major components, not just accelerators, implying a step-function increase in cooling infrastructure cost per rack.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Liquid cooling component suppliers — cold plates, manifolds, quick-disconnects — face a structural demand expansion beyond just GPU cooling modules.
insights.quote: this will all be liquid-cooled, so expect large internal liquid cooling blocks to be on everything

insights.claim: Next-generation AI servers at the Wiwynn Computex 2026 booth include rear connectors for scale-up networking within the rack, signaling that high-bandwidth in-rack interconnects are being integrated at the server hardware level rather than as external add-ons.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: In-rack scale-up networking vendors and NIC suppliers should expect tighter co-design requirements with server OEMs for next-gen AI platforms.
insights.quote: There is a new liquid-cooling nozzle and connectors for scale-up networking within the rack.
